hook.text: Cut one cent per unit per month. After a year, that's twelve cents—multiplied by millions of units.

buildup.text: Kaizen costing pursues continuous, incremental cost reductions during the production phase.

discovery.text: Workers on the floor suggest small improvements—a better layout, fewer steps, less waste.

twist.text: No single change is dramatic, but compounded over years, the savings are enormous.

climax.text: Toyota and other Japanese firms built global dominance partly through relentless kaizen discipline.

punchline.text: Small daily improvements add up to massive yearly savings.

quiz.question: What is the core idea of kaizen costing?
options: Continuous small cost reductions over time
